Hong Kong authorities will set up a company this year to accelerate the development of an innovation hub near the border, with firms already expressing interest in moving into the San Tin Technopole, a senior tech official has said.

news.headline: Iraqis celebrates US-Iran ceasefire as two-week halt in war begins
news.summary: Celebrations have erupted in Iraq following the announcement of a two-week ceasefire between Iran and the US.

news.headline: Climate activist Greta Thunberg slams Trump’s threats against Iran
news.summary: Polls have found that young people in the United States are more sceptical of foreign intervention and war on Iran.

news.headline: Trump administration reaffirms plans to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia
news.summary: Abrego Garcia's wrongful removal in 2025 has spurred criticism of the Trump administration's mass deportation campaign.

news.headline: US journalist Shelly Kittleson released after kidnap in Iraq, officials say
news.summary: US officials say they are now "working to support her safe departure from Iraq".

news.headline: Japan’s deportation drive strikes fear into asylum seekers, foreign residents
news.summary:
Japan’s tougher deportation drive is deepening fear among asylum seekers and long-term foreign residents, rights groups say, warning that the government’s push to remove more undocumented migrants is clashing with its duty to protect refugees.

news.headline: Chinese youth are embracing Ching Ming with cultural confidence
news.summary:
The Ching Ming Festival, a traditional Chinese festival with a long history, is a time dedicated to paying tribute to deceased family members and ancestors. For me, it is quiet, restrained and deeply personal.

news.headline: Oil prices slide, stocks surge as Trump announces two-week Iran ceasefire
news.summary: Investors welcome the possible resumption of oil and gas flows through the Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: Singapore says no negotiations for Hormuz safe passage; transit is ‘right, not privilege’
news.summary:
Singapore will not negotiate for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz, as doing so would undermine fundamental principles of international law, Foreign Affairs Minister Vivian Balakrishnan has said.

news.headline: Tributes after British teenager dies while visiting Vietnam
news.summary: Orla Wates, reportedly killed in a road accident, is remembered by her mother as "beautiful, independent and very funny".

news.headline: U.S. journalist Shelly Kittleson, kidnapped in Iraq, is freed in prisoner swap
news.summary: Kittleson, a freelancer for several U.S. outlets, was seized last week in Baghdad by Kataib Hezbollah, a Shiite militia aligned with Iran.

news.headline: Iran says talks with US will begin in Pakistan’s Islamabad on Friday
news.summary: Tehran says the negotiations will be based on its 10-point proposal, which calls for control over Strait of Hormuz.

news.headline: Iran agrees to open Strait of Hormuz for two-week US ceasefire
news.summary: Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says ‘if attacks against Iran are halted,’ then Iran agrees to a US ceasefire.
